question: Based on the chart, what can be concluded about water sales when temperature reaches 300°F?

options.A: Cannot be determined due to data variability.
options.B: Water sales will definitely be very low at 300°F.
options.C: Water sales will definitely continue to decrease at 300°F following the same pattern.
options.D: Water sales will definitely be higher than at any lower temperature.

explanation: While the trend line suggests decreasing water sold, the data shows significant variability and only goes up to 255°F. Extrapolating to 300°F involves uncertainty, and option C implies a level of certainty that cannot be guaranteed given the scatter in the data. The concealed uncertainty lies in the variability around the trend line and the act of extrapolation beyond the observed data range.
